mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points over two months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production below its 6-month moving average signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant negative skew during industrial production contractions, or the signal reverses to positive during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real interest rates, defined as the federal funds rate exceeding the annualized CPI growth rate by more than 1.5 percentage points, compress equity valuations and suppress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns dominate when real rates rise, or the spread metric shows zero predictive power across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year period, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.0% signals immediate economic stagnation, prompting institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of low-GDP-growth quarters, or the signal fails to predict direction when inflation is above 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production and payroll growth, where production rises while employment falls, indicates productivity-driven margin expansion that boosts equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal predicts negative returns or shows no statistical edge, or the mechanism breaks down when energy prices spike above 10% YoY.
